members.member: Zipper is an immutable persistent data structure, to represent collection with single focused (selected, active) element. Consist of REVERSED `Left` immutable list, `Focus` element, and the `Right` immutable list. That's why a Zipper name, where left and right part are joined / zipped in focus item.
Empty singleton instance to start building your zipper
True is zipper does not contain items
Index of Focus item, from `0` to `Count-1`
Number of items
Left REVERSED list, so the Head of the list is just prior the Focus item
Right list, where Head is just after the Focus item
Single focus item
Sets a new focus and pushes the old focus to the Left list.
Sets a new focus and pushes the old focus to the Left list.
Sets a new focus and pushes the old focus to the right list.
Sets a new focus and pushes the old focus to the right list.
Removes a focus, filling the hole with the item from the left list, or from the right if the left is empty
Removes a focus, filling the hole with the item from the right list, or from the left if the right is empty
Shifts focus one element to the left (decrementing its Index).
Shifts focus one element to the right (incrementing its Index).
Sets a new focus and returns a new zipper with the left and right lists unchanged
Maps over the zipper items producing a new zipper
Maps over the zipper items with item index, producing a new zipper
Other ImZipper methods
Appends array items to zipper
Converts to array.
Shifts focus to a specified index, e.g. a random access
Updates a focus element if it is present, otherwise does nothing. If the focus item is the equal one, then returns the same zipper back.
Update the item at random index, by shifting and updating it
Update the item at random index, by shifting and updating it
Folds zipper to a single value
Folds zipper to a single value by using an item index

member.summary: The count of entries in the map

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap`2.GetEntryOrNull(System.Int32)
member.summary: Lookup for the entry by hash. The returned entry maybe either single entry or the `HashConflictEntry` with multiple key-value entries for the same hash. If hash does not match the method returns `null`

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap`2.GetSurePresentEntry(System.Int32)
member.summary: Assumes the hash is present in the map (because the map is immutable if we know the hash is present then it cannot disappear) otherwise the result. Returns the Entry with the `hash` though it may be a `HashConflictingEntry`

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap`2.AddOrGetEntry(System.Int32,ImTools.ImHashMap{`0,`1}.Entry)
member.summary: Returns the found entry with the same hash or the new map with added new entry. Note that the empty map will return the entry the same as if the entry was found - so the consumer should check for the empty map. Note that the method cannot return the `null` - when the existing entry is not found it will always be the new map with the added entry.

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap`2.ReplaceEntry(ImTools.ImHashMap{`0,`1}.Entry,ImTools.ImHashMap{`0,`1}.Entry)
member.summary: Returns the new map with old entry replaced by the new entry. Note that the old entry should be present.

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap`2.RemoveEntry(ImTools.ImHashMap{`0,`1}.Entry)
member.summary: Removes the certainly present old entry and returns the new map without it.

members.member: this will have a different implementation for KVEntry and HashConflictingEntry, for the VEntry returning `this` is correct as for the map

member.name: M:ImTools.ImHashMap.ForEach``3(ImTools.ImHashMap{``0,``1},``2,System.Action{ImTools.ImHashMapEntry{``0,``1},System.Int32,``2},ImTools.MapParentStack)
member.summary: Depth-first in-order of hash traversal as described in http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tree_traversal. The `parents` parameter allows to reuse the stack memory used for the traversal between multiple calls. So you may pass the empty `parents` into the first `Enumerate` and then keep passing the same `parents` into the subsequent calls

member.name: T:ImTools.PartitionedHashMap
member.summary: The fixed array of maps (partitions) where the key first (lower) bits are used to locate the partition to lookup into. Note: The partition array is NOT immutable and operates by swapping the updated partition with the new one. The number of partitions may be specified by user or you can use the default number 16. The default number 16 was selected to be not so big to pay for the few items and not so small to diminish the use of partitions.

member.name: F:JasperFx.AutoCreate.None

member.name: M:JasperFx.Core.TimeSpanExtensions.ToDisplay(System.TimeSpan)

member.name: M:JasperFx.Core.TimeSpanExtensions.TimespanRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\n^(?<quantity>\\d+ # quantity is expressed as some digits\n(\\.\\d+)?) # optionally followed by a decimal point or colon and more digits\n\\s* # optional whitespace\n(?<units>[a-z]*) # units is expressed as a word\n$ # match the entire string
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnorePatternWhitespace
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ "quantity" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ "units" capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [a-z] atomically any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.
